title: Landolphia gummifera
uses: Landolphia gummifera is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It has been reported to have antimalarial properties and is used to treat malaria. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In addition, its sap is used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The medicinal properties of Landolphia gummifera are still being researched for their potential benefits and applications.
taxonomy: Landolphia gummifera is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family is part of the Apiales order and consists of over 5,000 species. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into 145 genera, with Landolphia being one of them. Landolphia gummifera is native to the tropical regions of Africa and is a deciduous shrub or small tree. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants that have milky sap and showy flowers.

side_effects: Landolphia gummifera, also known as the African chewing stick, has been reported to cause irritation and inflammation in some individuals due to its high latex content. The latex can also cause an allergic reaction in some people, leading to symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling.
